details.text: Derivative: الطُّورِ means the mountain and refers to the mountain in which Allah spoke to Musa (as).

Derivative: تَسِيرُ from سَارَ – سَيْر. سَير (Sair) in Arabic refers to an easy motion or effortless smooth movement. Here in reference to the mountains it means to float or sail away in an easy effortless manner.
Derivative: خَوْضٍ originally means to wade into water by walking into it. It then came to be used to refer to wading or delving into any false or idle matter.
Derivative: هَنِيئًا means at ease, in comfort and without concern satisfying oneself.
Derivative: مَسْطُورٍ – means to be put in lines i.e., written down.
Derivative: أَلَتْنَا from أَلَتَ – يَألِت – أَلْتًا means to deduct from or give someone less than they deserve in recompense or a right.

Derivative: رَهِينٌ something that is retained or held in collateral, as a pledge or security for something. It is something that you are reliant or dependent upon to secure something. Here, the good deeds are someone’s collateral or security to enter paradise.
Derivative: يَتَنَازَعُونَ means to contest with one another and here it implies the passing around of cups in a playful manner.
Derivative: تَأْثِيمٌ
Derivative: تَأْثِيمٌ – إِثْمٌ (Ithm) means a sin and تَأْثِيمٌ means that which leads to or causes sin.
Derivative: السَّمُومِ is a burning hot wind.
Derivative: الْبَرُّ is an attribute of Allah which means the source of all goodness and beneficence.
Derivative: رَقٍّ refers to a parchment and paper. It comes from رَقِيق (Raqeeq) which means something that is delicate, thin and fine.
Derivative: الْمَنُونِ refers to time “الدَّهْر” (Ad-Dahr).
- Literally means the “doubt or uncertainty of time” i.e., we will wait to see “what comes of him in time”.
Derivative: أَحْلَامُ is the plural of حِلْمٌ and among its meanings is the عَقْل (‘Aql – mind, reasoning or intellect).
Derivative: تَقَوَّلَ – قَالَ means “he said” and تَقَوَّلَ means he made something up in speech.

Derivative: الْمَعْمُورِ refers to a place that is well-populated and visited meaning that there are many people or angels there depending on whether it is referring to the الْبَيْتِ الْمَعْمُورِ (Al-Bayt ul-Ma’moor) in the seventh heaven which is frequented by Angels or the Bait ul-Ma’moor of the earth which is Masjid Al-Haram which is frequented by those performing Hajj and ‘Umrah.
Derivative: مَغْرَمٍ refers to the financial penalty i.e., a cost or debt in this context.
Derivative: مُثْقَلُونَ from أَثْقَلَ which means to place a burden (literally something heavy) upon someone. مُثْقَل (Muthqal) is the one who has had a burden placed upon him.
Derivative: إِدْبَارَ from أَدْبَرَ which means to show or turn one’s back or to retreat. Here it refers to the “fading away or retreat” of the stars.
Derivative: الْمَسْجُورِ from سَجَرَ which means to heat up. الْمَسْجُورِ can mean filled with fire or heat where it boils up and rises.

Derivative: تَمُورُ refers to a side to side or back and forth movement indicative of instability and a quick shaking or wavering movement like a vibration on a major scale as it is in reference to the sky.
